food_subgroup: Breaded and fried fish burger meal with fries and beverage

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has 'fish sandwich' (FOODON_00002383) as the closest match; no specific entry for double fish fillet burger meal with fries and drink.
ontology.foodex2_description: Fish burger (breaded fish fillet in bun)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers fish burger; no specific code for double-patty upsized meal combo. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept for this commercial fast-food meal combo.

serving.description: 1 upsized meal (double fish burger + large fries + large drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One upsized meal consisting of a double Filet-O-Fish burger (approximately 200 g), a large portion of french fries (approximately 150 g), and a large carbonated soft drink (approximately 400 ml / 400 g), totalling approximately 750 g.

nutrient_source.title: McDonald's Singapore Nutrition Information — Filet-O-Fish and French Fries; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Fish fillet, battered or breaded, and fried; Carbonated beverage cola
nutrient_source.organization: McDonald's Singapore / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values per 100 g are estimated by combining McDonald's Singapore published nutrition data for the Filet-O-Fish burger (single), large fries, and a standard cola drink in upsized proportions, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for breaded fried fish fillet (FDC ID 175139), fast-food french fries (FDC ID 172004), and cola beverage (FDC ID 174851). The double burger component doubles the fish-patty contribution. Weighted average per 100 g of the total meal (approx. 750 g) is computed. Values are estimates; exact McDonald's Singapore formulation may differ slightly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — white bread and fast-food french fries analogous estimates; Atkinson FS et al. Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Double Filet-O-Fish Upsized Meal as a composite. GI estimated as weighted average of component GI values: steamed white-flour bun (GI ~71, analogous to white bread), breaded fried fish fillet (GI ~38, analogous to fish fingers per Sydney GI Database), french fries (GI ~63 per Sydney GI Database, Atkinson 2021), and cola beverage (GI ~63 per Sydney GI Database). Weighted by carbohydrate contribution of each component in the meal. Composite GI estimated at 63 (medium). GL per serving = (63 × 165 g carbohydrate in full meal) / 100 = 30.3, where 165 g carbohydrate is derived from 750 g meal × 22 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (310 mg per 100 g; approximately 2325 mg per full upsized meal)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this meal, as a single serving provides approximately 97–116% of the WHO-recommended daily sodium limit of 2000–2400 mg, primarily from the tartar sauce, processed cheese, seasoned fries, and cola.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (GL ~30 per serving) and high refined carbohydrate content from white bun, battered coating, french fries, and sugary cola
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this meal or consume it only occasionally with careful carbohydrate budgeting, as the high glycemic load (~30 per serving) and approximately 165 g of rapidly digestible carbohydrates will cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combination of saturated fat, trans fat, high sodium, and high refined sugar from cola
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this meal due to its saturated fat (~15.75 g per meal), trans fat (~0.75 g per meal), high sodium (~2325 mg per meal), and added sugars from the cola, all of which are established risk factors for adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Double Filet-O-Fish Upsized Meal is a McDonald's Singapore fast-food combo meal consisting of a double Filet-O-Fish burger, a large portion of french fries, and a large carbonated soft drink. The Filet-O-Fish burger features two deep-fried, breaded Alaskan pollock white-fish fillets, each topped with a half-slice of processed cheese and a dollop of creamy tartar sauce, served in a soft steamed white-flour bun. The double variant stacks two fish patties for a more substantial fish-forward bite. The fries are McDonald's signature thin-cut, salted french fries, deep-fried to a crisp golden exterior with a fluffy interior. The meal is served upsized, meaning large-portion fries and a large drink. The overall flavour profile is savoury and mildly tangy from the tartar sauce, with a crispy-tender fish texture contrasting with the soft bun. Nutritionally, this is a high-energy, high-sodium, high-carbohydrate ultra-processed meal. Per 100 g the meal provides approximately 175 kcal, 5.2 g protein, 7.8 g fat, and 22 g carbohydrate. A full upsized meal (~750 g) delivers approximately 1313 kcal, 39 g protein, 58.5 g fat, and 165 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is very high at approximately 2325 mg per meal, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or Type 2 Diabetes. The glycemic load is high (~30 per serving), driven by the white bun, breaded coating, fries, and cola. The meal is certified Halal in Singapore. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with fish, gluten, milk, egg, or soy allergies.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Double Filet-O-Fish Upsized Meal?
Is the Double Filet-O-Fish Meal halal in Singapore?
What fish is used in McDonald's Filet-O-Fish?
Is the Double Filet-O-Fish Meal healthy?
How much sodium is in a McDonald's fish burger meal?
Can diabetics eat the Filet-O-Fish Meal?
What is the glycemic index of a McDonald's fish burger?
Double fish burger McDonald's Singapore calories

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are estimated by combining McDonald's Singapore published nutrition data (where available) with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy reference values for component foods (breaded fried fish fillet, fast-food french fries, cola beverage), weighted by approximate serving proportions. No single approved primary source provides exact per-100 g values for this specific composite meal. GI is an analogous estimate from component GI values sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates for the full upsized meal (double fish burger ~200 g + large fries ~150 g + large cola ~400 g = ~750 g total). Per-100 g values are weighted averages across all components. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entries used for this composite estimate. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported consistently across the component sources used. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources used. 5. Trans fat is present at a low level (~0.1 g per 100 g) from the deep-frying process; this is a low but non-zero value consistent with USDA data for fried fast-food items. 6. GI is an analogous composite estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific meal exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 7. The meal is listed as Halal-certified at McDonald's Singapore outlets. 8. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1 through b12, folate, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data and are approximate.
